grader_results.details: score=4.0/5 (min=3.0): The annotation studio implements 8 of 9 required features with functional CRUD operations, though form validation could be more robust with consistent Zod schema usage. | Findings: [low] src/app/golden-sets/[id]/page.tsx: Bulk import feature exists as ImportDialog component but implementation details not visible in main page code; [medium] src/app/conventions/new/page.tsx: Form validation relies on client-side toast for error handling rather than integrated Zod schema validation; [low] src/app/layout.tsx: Navigation sidebar not visible in provided layout file

[cached] score=5.0/5 (min=2.0): Excellent implementation of all 6 comparison and trend analysis features with comprehensive functionality including side-by-side comparison, regression detection, trend charts with statistical analysis, baseline selection, and visual alerting. | Findings: [low] src/app/runs/compare/page.tsx: Minor accessibility improvement: comparison controls lack proper ARIA labels; [low] src/components/runs/trend-chart.tsx: Chart component could benefit from keyboard navigation support

[cached] score=4.0/5 (min=3.0): Well-structured dashboard with 7/8 required features implemented, missing only a truly prominent latest run score display and has some error handling gaps. | Findings: [medium] Lines 27-44: Missing error handling for database queries - all DB operations use .all() or .get() without try/catch blocks; [low] Lines 59-103: Latest run score not prominently displayed as a dedicated card - it's buried in the Eval Runs summary card description; [low] Lines 105-172: Engineering Quality section only appears conditionally based on hasEngineering flag, reducing at-a-glance consistency; [low] Lines 213-236: Recent runs list shows run_id in monospace font which may be less readable than a formatted display name

score=1.0/5 (min=2.0): Critical security vulnerabilities in webhook endpoint with no authentication, validation, or data integrity checks - completely unsuitable for production fintech use. | Findings: [critical] src/app/api/runs/webhook/route.ts: Webhook endpoint has no authentication - accepts any POST request without bearer token, HMAC signature, or API key validation; [critical] src/app/api/runs/webhook/route.ts:lines 14-30: No payload validation - directly inserts untrusted JSON into database without schema validation; [high] src/app/api/runs/webhook/route.ts: No duplicate run_id handling - will create duplicate entries for the same run_id; [high] src/app/api/runs/webhook/route.ts: No transaction safety - run and task_results are inserted separately without transaction wrapping; [medium] src/app/api/runs/: No UI import endpoint - users cannot upload/import JSON eval runs except via webhook

score=4.0/5 (min=2.0): Postmortem workflow is 80% complete with display, API, linkage, and history features implemented, but lacks a creation form UI. | Findings: [medium] src/app/runs/[id]/page.tsx: No UI form for creating postmortems despite having backend API support

[cached] score=5.0/5 (min=3.0): Excellent implementation with all 8 required features present and well-implemented, including run list, detail view, gate matrix, task results, severity grouping, engineering findings, score visualization, and dark mode support. | Findings: [low] src/components/runs/run-detail.tsx:336: Truncated code in GraderDetailsCell function - appears to be cut off mid-expression
